headline: Texas Rolls; UCLA's Rise Reshapes the Power Poll
subhead: Week 5 saw Texas cement its top ranking with a blowout, while UCLA's demolition of USC forced a major poll reshuffling.
lead: Texas delivered the week's most emphatic statement, dismantling Arkansas 49–3 to stay unbeaten and reinforce its claim atop the PCFL. But the more intriguing story was UCLA's 38–9 dismantling of crosstown rival USC, a result so lopsided it vaulted the Bruins directly into the top four and raised urgent questions about USC's offensive identity.

segments.title: Texas Leaves No Doubt at the Top
segments.body: A 46-point margin against an opponent rated fifth in the power poll is the kind of scoreline that silences skepticism. Texas's offense was relentless, its defense suffocating, and there was no moment of vulnerability. The coaching room got everything right, and the result was less a game than a coronation.

segments.title: UCLA's Stunning Takeover of the Crosstown Rivalry
segments.body: USC entered as a ranked program with postseason aspirations; UCLA left it 38–9 in the dust. This was not a close call that broke wrong for the Trojans—it was systematic dominance on both sides of scrimmage. UCLA's ascent into the top four is now justified by execution, not projection.

segments.title: Michigan's Secondary Exposed, Boston College Pounces
segments.body: A 36–9 loss at home signals more than a bad day; it suggests structural vulnerability in pass coverage that Boston College exploited without mercy. Michigan's defense gave up far too much vertical space, and the Eagles' receivers found soft pockets repeatedly. This is the kind of film that opponents will study and replicate.

segments.title: Tennessee's High-Wire Act Over Miami
segments.body: Fifty points in a win shows offensive firepower, but yielding 34 at home reveals a secondary that can be carved up. Tennessee's staff made enough plays down the stretch to hold on, but this was closer to a barn-burner than a rout. Miami proved it belongs in the conversation even in defeat.

segments.title: Close Calls in the Midfield Crowd
segments.body: Oregon's 34–28 win over Clemson was a six-point statement in a tight race for tournament positioning. Neither team is out of it, but both now have material on tape about where they stand. The margin was thin enough to sting and wide enough to matter.

closer: Week 6 will test whether Texas and Tennessee can maintain momentum against ranked opponents, while UCLA faces its biggest test yet to prove this week was the start of a run, not a mirage.
